title: ostree: Cannot handle metadata object bigger than 10.5 MB
body:
We use skopeo + ostree to run colin[1] for sanity checking of images in unprivileged containers in openshift.

We recently hit an issue with "maximum metadata size" with skopeo+ostree.

I am not sure whether limitation is in skopeo or in ostree.
```
[user@host ~]$ /usr/bin/skopeo copy docker-archive:/var/tmp/huge_image.tar ostree:huge_image@/var/tmp/ostree
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:366de6e5861ac8e9dab3ba7c11f61fd031ca7406689315873e7d1cf7f0714262
 200.92 MB / 200.92 MB [====================================================] 1s
Copying blob sha256:00860a9b126fa4deaff64c3ff44c02565ba363bf1c953b97e9012e48637bd5c0
 10.00 KB / 10.00 KB [======================================================] 0s
Copying blob sha256:4376391867eca969edd52c8343f657b71c425d1bf665d018c8eee524bdbd5306
 2.07 GB / 2.07 GB [=======================================================] 15s
Copying config sha256:a1f186814218ee8d2adbc9d264b72098789764e9697a361468ca3bbed1cd3232
 5.17 KB / 5.17 KB [========================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
FATA[0148] Error committing the finished image: /builddir/build/BUILD/skopeo-e3034e1d91056b480b28a5ae7c56526e084588cf/vendor/src/github.com/ostreedev/ostree-go/pkg/otbuiltin/commit.go:407 - Metadata object of type 'commit' is 13.8 MB; maximum metadata size is 10.5 MB
```

There is even fedora BZ for this issue with more detailed reproducer.
https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1628121.

[1] https://github.com/user-cont/colin

comments.body: https://github.com/ostreedev/ostree/blob/a70d2f67310bd869c6a40c4013dc51a3e2f2d2a8/src/libostree/ostree-repo-commit.c#L2315 is where the error is being reached within `metadata_size_valid()`.

OK, related comment says that it is arbitrary number. [1]
I assume it is caused but huge layer in image because OSBS merge all layers into single one.

```
sh-4.4#  tar -tvf /var/tmp/huge_image.tar 
-r--r--r-- 0/0       210677760 1969-12-31 19:00 366de6e5861ac8e9dab3ba7c11f61fd031ca7406689315873e7d1cf7f0714262.tar
-r--r--r-- 0/0           10240 1969-12-31 19:00 00860a9b126fa4deaff64c3ff44c02565ba363bf1c953b97e9012e48637bd5c0.tar
-r--r--r-- 0/0      2222673920 1969-12-31 19:00 4376391867eca969edd52c8343f657b71c425d1bf665d018c8eee524bdbd5306.tar
-r--r--r-- 0/0            5291 1969-12-31 19:00 a1f186814218ee8d2adbc9d264b72098789764e9697a361468ca3bbed1cd3232.json
l--------- 0/0               0 1969-12-31 19:00 366de6e5861ac8e9dab3ba7c11f61fd031ca7406689315873e7d1cf7f0714262/layer.tar -> ../366de6e5861ac8e9dab3ba7c11f61fd031ca7406689315873e7d1cf7f0714262.tar
-r--r--r-- 0/0               3 1969-12-31 19:00 366de6e5861ac8e9dab3ba7c11f61fd031ca7406689315873e7d1cf7f0714262/VERSION
-r--r--r-- 0/0              73 1969-12-31 19:00 366de6e5861ac8e9dab3ba7c11f61fd031ca7406689315873e7d1cf7f0714262/json
l--------- 0/0               0 1969-12-31 19:00 f882c9143c55d7467087a60ff6e98854a4a628e0131248e1c6b066dce4d1e55a/layer.tar -> ../00860a9b126fa4deaff64c3ff44c02565ba363bf1c953b97e9012e48637bd5c0.tar
-r--r--r-- 0/0               3 1969-12-31 19:00 f882c9143c55d7467087a60ff6e98854a4a628e0131248e1c6b066dce4d1e55a/VERSION
-r--r--r-- 0/0             149 1969-12-31 19:00 f882c9143c55d7467087a60ff6e98854a4a628e0131248e1c6b066dce4d1e55a/json
l--------- 0/0               0 1969-12-31 19:00 86706558905a844e0e43549296a31444692a0c6d0da6feb78ea9aaf2d1ecaa4a/layer.tar -> ../4376391867eca969edd52c8343f657b71c425d1bf665d018c8eee524bdbd5306.tar
-r--r--r-- 0/0               3 1969-12-31 19:00 86706558905a844e0e43549296a31444692a0c6d0da6feb78ea9aaf2d1ecaa4a/VERSION
-r--r--r-- 0/0            5011 1969-12-31 19:00 86706558905a844e0e43549296a31444692a0c6d0da6feb78ea9aaf2d1ecaa4a/json
-r--r--r-- 0/0               2 1969-12-31 19:00 repositories
-r--r--r-- 0/0             322 1969-12-31 19:00 manifest.json
``` 
```
sh-4.4# tar -xf /var/tmp/huge_image.tar 4376391867eca969edd52c8343f657b71c425d1bf665d018c8eee524bdbd5306.tar
sh-4.4# ls -lh 4376391867eca969edd52c8343f657b71c425d1bf665d018c8eee524bdbd5306.tar
-r--r--r--. 1 root root 2.1G Dec 31  1969 4376391867eca969edd52c8343f657b71c425d1bf665d018c8eee524bdbd5306.tar
sh-4.4# tar -tvf 4376391867eca969edd52c8343f657b71c425d1bf665d018c8eee524bdbd5306.tar | wc -l
144713
```
Is that assumption correct? BTW Is it related to how skopeo stores data in ostree or is that pure ostree limitation?

@cgwalters, could it cause any ABI issues if the value will be increased?

[1] https://github.com/ostreedev/ostree/blob/a70d2f67310bd869c6a40c4013dc51a3e2f2d2a8/src/libostree/ostree-core.h#L39

> BTW Is it related to how skopeo stores data in ostree or is that pure ostree limitation?

If I had to _guess_, it’s caused by storing the whole tarsplit blob (= all headers of the tar stream of a layer, in their unmodified binary form) in the per-layer commit objects; that can indeed grow with the image size. Otherwise the commit object seem to be fairly small if I understand the OSTree docs right.

(I have no idea whether there is any better option for storing the tarsplit blobs.)

yes I think it depends on the tarsplit blob which in this case probably exceeds 10 MB.  I agree with Colin ( (https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1628121#c2) that it is probably better to drop this limitation in OSTree instead of trying to include the tarsplit data as part of the OCI layer data.
this has been already solved upstream
